rationale: The cited paper is used with a specific functional role, not just listed in background citations. The citing article explicitly contrasts its contribution against Wilcox et al. by stating it studies conspicuous use of authentic brands and observer inferences, rather than counterfeit luxury purchase choice. It also draws on Wilcox et al.'s finding about impression-management motives in counterfeit use to frame boundary-condition discussion in the General Discussion.
relation_description: The citing paper positions itself as moving from Wilcox et al.'s counterfeit-consumption context to authentic-brand usage, focusing on how observers react to conspicuous use and brand dilution. It uses Wilcox et al.'s motivation finding as a substantive comparison point when discussing how responses to authentic versus counterfeit conspicuous use may differ.
